title: initialize() fetches the action log THEN subscribes to Realtime, dropping any action committed in the gap and permanently stalling on the seq hole
evidence: initialize() first does the historical fetch `await this.supabase.from('room_actions').select('*')...order('seq')` (lines 210-214) and sets `nextExpectedSeq = existingActions.length`, and ONLY afterwards calls `.subscribe(...)` (line 278) to start receiving INSERT broadcasts. Any row committed by another client between the select completing and the channel actually becoming SUBSCRIBED is never delivered (no live subscription yet) and is not in the replayed snapshot. The first SUBSCRIBED does NOT resync: `const recovered = this._everSubscribed && this._connection !== 'connected'` (line 283) is false on first subscribe (`_everSubscribed` starts false), so `resyncLog()` is skipped. A later action arriving via Realtime (seq = missed+1) is buffered by shouldBufferSeq but flushPendingActions can never reach it because the exact `nextExpectedSeq` (the missed seq) is absent — the engine wedges on the gap.
impact: A turn taken during the fetch->subscribe window is silently lost for the joining/reloading client; its engine stalls on the missing seq. Recovery only occurs on a Realtime reconnect (which triggers resyncLog) or a manual reload; otherwise the board freezes and the user is left with only the turn-stall banner (after 30s) offering leave-to-lobby. Race window widens with log fetch latency on late joiners.
recommendation: Establish the Realtime subscription BEFORE the historical fetch (buffer live events during replay), or unconditionally run resyncLog() once immediately after the first SUBSCRIBED (drop the `recovered` guard for the initial subscribe) so any actions committed during the fetch/subscribe gap are caught up deterministically.
acceptance_criteria: An action committed after the initialize() select() but before the channel is SUBSCRIBED is applied by the client without a manual reload
A harness or test simulates a commit during the fetch/subscribe window and asserts nextExpectedSeq advances past it
